articles.title: Scientists Discover New Method to Remove Microplastics from Oceans
articles.summary: Researchers at MIT have developed a groundbreaking filter technology that can remove up to 99% of microplastics from seawater. The device uses a special magnetic material that attracts plastic particles without harming marine life. Scientists believe this innovation could be deployed on ships within the next three years. Environmental groups have praised the discovery as a major step forward in fighting ocean pollution. Governments in Europe and Asia have already expressed interest in funding large-scale trials.

vocabulary.definition: tiny plastic particles smaller than 5mm found in the environment
vocabulary.example: Microplastics have been found even in the deepest parts of the ocean.

vocabulary.definition: innovative and important; never done before
vocabulary.example: The groundbreaking research changed how we treat the disease.

vocabulary.definition: to put something into use or service
vocabulary.example: The army was deployed to help with disaster relief.

vocabulary.definition: a test to see how well something works
vocabulary.example: The new drug is currently in clinical trials.

articles.question: What percentage of microplastics can the new filter technology remove from seawater?

articles.title: AI Tools Help Doctors Detect Early-Stage Cancer More Accurately
articles.summary: A new artificial intelligence system developed by Google DeepMind has shown remarkable accuracy in detecting early-stage cancers from medical scans. In clinical trials involving over 50,000 patients, the AI outperformed human radiologists in identifying tumors in their earliest, most treatable stages. The system analyzes CT scans and MRIs in seconds, compared to hours for human doctors. Medical experts say this could dramatically improve survival rates for patients in countries with limited access to specialist doctors. The technology is expected to receive regulatory approval in the US and UK by the end of the year.

vocabulary.definition: a doctor who specializes in reading medical images like X-rays and MRI scans
vocabulary.example: The radiologist studied the X-ray carefully before giving her diagnosis.

vocabulary.definition: an abnormal growth of cells in the body, which can be cancerous
vocabulary.example: The tumor was discovered early, which made treatment much easier.

vocabulary.definition: official permission from a government body to use a product or service
vocabulary.example: The new vaccine received regulatory approval after successful trials.

vocabulary.definition: to do better than someone or something else
vocabulary.example: The electric car outperformed petrol cars in the fuel efficiency test.

articles.question: How does the AI system's speed compare to human doctors when analyzing medical scans?

articles.title: Japan Announces Ambitious Plan to Become Carbon Neutral by 2040
articles.summary: Japan's government has announced an accelerated plan to achieve carbon neutrality ten years earlier than its previous target of 2050. The new strategy includes tripling the country's solar energy capacity, investing heavily in hydrogen fuel technology, and phasing out coal power plants by 2032. Prime Minister Hiroshi Tanaka called it the most significant environmental commitment in Japan's modern history. The plan is expected to create over two million new jobs in the renewable energy sector. Critics, however, argue that the targets are too ambitious and warn of potential energy shortages during the transition period.

vocabulary.definition: producing no net carbon dioxide emissions into the atmosphere
vocabulary.example: The company aims to be carbon neutral by switching to renewable energy.

vocabulary.definition: to gradually stop using or producing something
vocabulary.example: The government plans to phase out single-use plastics within five years.

vocabulary.definition: energy from sources that are naturally replenished, like solar or wind
vocabulary.example: Renewable energy now powers 30% of the country's electricity grid.

vocabulary.definition: requiring a lot of effort and determination to achieve
vocabulary.example: The startup had ambitious plans to expand into 50 countries within two years.

articles.question: By what year does Japan plan to phase out its coal power plants under the new strategy?

articles.title: Global Space Agency Plans First Permanent Moon Base Construction
articles.summary: An international coalition of space agencies, including NASA, ESA, and JAXA, has announced plans to begin construction of a permanent lunar base by 2031. The facility, named Artemis Base Camp, will house up to twelve astronauts at a time and serve as a launching point for future missions to Mars. Robots and autonomous machines will begin preparing the site in 2028, three years before human crews arrive. Scientists say the moon's south pole was chosen for the base because of confirmed water ice deposits in permanently shadowed craters. The project will cost an estimated 200 billion dollars and involve contributions from more than 20 countries.

vocabulary.definition: a group of different organizations or countries working together
vocabulary.example: A coalition of environmental groups lobbied the government for stricter pollution laws.

vocabulary.definition: able to work or operate independently without human control
vocabulary.example: Autonomous vehicles use sensors to navigate without a human driver.

vocabulary.definition: relating to the moon
vocabulary.example: The lunar surface is covered with fine grey dust called regolith.

vocabulary.definition: a natural accumulation of a substance found in the ground or on a surface
vocabulary.example: Large deposits of gold were discovered in the mountains.

articles.question: Why was the moon's south pole chosen as the location for the Artemis Base Camp?

articles.title: Record Number of Young People Learning Foreign Languages Online
articles.summary: A new global report shows that online language learning has reached an all-time high, with over 600 million active users on digital platforms in 2026. English remains the most studied language worldwide, followed by Spanish, Mandarin, and Japanese. The report highlights that younger generations aged 18-35 account for nearly 70% of all language learners. Experts attribute the surge to increased international job opportunities and the popularity of foreign media, especially Korean and Japanese entertainment. Language learning apps now use AI to personalize lessons and provide real-time pronunciation feedback, making them significantly more effective than traditional methods.

vocabulary.definition: to say that something is caused by a particular reason
vocabulary.example: Scientists attribute the rise in temperatures to increased greenhouse gas emissions.

vocabulary.definition: a sudden and large increase
vocabulary.example: There was a surge in demand for face masks during the pandemic.

vocabulary.definition: to design or change something to suit one specific person's needs
vocabulary.example: The app personalizes your workout plan based on your fitness goals.

vocabulary.definition: the way in which a word or language is spoken
vocabulary.example: Her pronunciation improved dramatically after six months of speaking practice.

articles.question: What two factors do experts say are driving the surge in foreign language learning among young people?
